name: Béznar Reservoir Circular
name_original: Embalse de Béznar — circular desde Restábal
description: This difficult 12.0-mile touring cycling route around Embalse de Béznar from Restábal offers stunning reservoir views and charming village s
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around El Valle are set within the caldera of an inactive volcano, offering a distinct landscape. The region features a relatively flat valley floor suitable for cycling, transitioning into rolling hills and cloud forests at higher elevations. Situated at 600 meters, El Valle experiences a cooler climate, providing comfortable conditions for outdoor activities. This unique geological setting offers diverse terrain for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around El Valle The most popular touring cycling route is Béznar Reservoir Circular, a 12.0 miles (19.3 km) difficult trail that takes 1 hour 28 minutes to complete, offering views of the reservoir and significant elevation gain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Alto del Legionario — circular from Albuñuelas, a difficult 30.5 miles (49.0 km) path. This route features challenging climbs and panoramic views of the surrounding area. Local touring cyclists also love the Metal Bridge – Presa de Béznar loop from El Pinar, a 24.8 miles (39.9 km) trail leading through varied terrain and past the Béznar reservoir, often completed in about 2 hours 54 minutes. Touring cycling around El Valle is defined by volcanic crater terrain, rolling hills, and cloud forests. The network offers options for various skill levels, from valley floor rides to more strenuous ascents. The routes in El Valle are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 40 reviews. More than 1,100 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore El Valle's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around El Valle?
A: There are over 80 touring cycling routes documented around El Valle on komoot. This includes a variety of options, from relatively flat rides within the volcanic caldera to more challenging ascents into the surrounding hills and cloud forests.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in El Valle?
A: Yes, El Valle offers 3 easy touring cycling routes, which are great for beginners or families. The relatively flat valley floor provides accessible terrain. While specific family-friendly routes are not detailed, these easier options are a good starting point for a relaxed ride.

Q: What are some challenging long-distance touring cycling routes in the region?
A: For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, El Valle has 67 difficult routes. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1045598856/alto-del-legionario-circular-from-albunuelas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto del Legionario — circular from Albuñuelas&lt;/a&gt;, a demanding 49.0 km path with significant climbs and panoramic views. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31897185" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nazari Bridge of Tablate – View of Restábal loop from El Valle&lt;/a&gt;, covering 52.6 km with over 1000 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes around El Valle?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in El Valle are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1040859139/beznar-reservoir-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Béznar Reservoir Circular&lt;/a&gt;, a 19.3 km difficult trail, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32067171" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Metal Bridge – Presa de Béznar loop from El Pinar&lt;/a&gt;, which is 39.9 km long.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along touring cycling routes in El Valle?
A: El Valle is rich in natural beauty. Cyclists can explore areas with picturesque waterfalls like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2777071" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lanjarón River Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4511116" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall of the Arroyo del Alcázar&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features unique geological formations such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3823223" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco de la Luna Gorge&lt;/a&gt; and the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4039932" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Padul&lt;/a&gt; lake. Many routes offer views of the volcanic crater terrain and lush cloud forests.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in El Valle?
A: El Valle enjoys a pleasant, cooler climate year-round due to its elevation of 600 meters, making it comfortable for outdoor activities. This 'spring-like' weather provides ideal conditions for touring cycling throughout the year, avoiding the intense heat of the Panamanian lowlands.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in El Valle?
A: The touring cycling routes in El Valle are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 40 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from the flat valley floor to challenging ascents, and the unique volcanic landscape that defines the region.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints accessible by bike in El Valle?
A: While many trails are primarily for hiking, touring cyclists can often reach areas that offer incredible panoramic views of the valley and its volcanic crater formation. Elevated points like Cerro Gaital (though requiring a hike to the summit) provide stunning vistas, and many cycling routes wind through hills that offer scenic perspectives of the lush landscape.

Q: Can I find cafes or places to rest along the touring cycling routes?
A: Cycling through El Valle allows for easy access to local amenities. While specific cafes on every route are not detailed, the town itself and various communities along the 'Ruta de la Caldera' offer opportunities for refreshments and rest stops. The vibrant Sunday Market in El Valle is also a great place to find local flavors.

Q: Are there options for multi-day touring cycling adventures in El Valle?
A: Given the variety of routes, including longer and more challenging options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10302947" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albuñuelas Village – Saleres loop from Albuñuelas&lt;/a&gt; (42.0 km), it is possible to plan multi-day touring cycling adventures. The region's diverse terrain and numerous attractions allow for extended exploration, with accommodations available in El Valle and surrounding areas.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling in El Valle?
A: El Valle offers a unique mix of terrain for touring cyclists. The town is nestled within a volcanic caldera, providing a relatively flat base for cycling. As you venture further, the landscape transitions into rolling, grassy hills and dense cloud forests, offering both gentle rides and more strenuous ascents with rewarding views.

Q: Are there any facilities or huts available along the routes for cyclists?
A: Yes, some areas around El Valle offer facilities that can be useful for cyclists. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6297091" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huerta de las Monjas Recreation Area&lt;/a&gt; provides amenities, and there are also options like a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/925724" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Shelter hut&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5440073" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Cañuelo Cabins and Spring&lt;/a&gt; that could serve as resting points or overnight stays for those on longer tours.
tips: Restábal is a small town located in the municipality of El Valle, in the Valle de Lecrín region. This town of Arab origin retains a special charm with its white houses and narrow, cobbled streets. The area has cycling routes that allow you to enjoy nature, explore the olive groves, and discover beautiful landscapes.

Beautiful perspective of the reservoir located on the Ízbor river, in the Lecrín valley. The dam was built with three objectives: to improve irrigation for farmers on the Costa Tropical, to supply water to the surrounding populations and to protect themselves from possible floods.

The road between Pinos del Valle and Restábal is a quiet route that runs near the Béznar Reservoir, in the province of Granada. With beautiful views of the reservoir and the Sierra Nevada, it is an ideal place for cycling enjoying nature and the tranquility of the surroundings.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as 'difficult' and requires 'very good fitness'. With a distance of 19.3 km and over 510 meters of elevation gain, it's best suited for experienced touring cyclists.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: On average, it takes about 1 hour and 28 minutes to complete the 19.3 km loop. However, this duration can vary depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route features mostly paved surfaces, including 13.1 km of 'Road', 4.38 km of 'State Road', and 1.81 km of 'Street'. Be aware that some segments may be unpaved and challenging, potentially requiring you to push your bike at times.

faqs.question: What are the main sights and viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter stunning views throughout the ride. Key highlights include a View of Restábal, the picturesque Béznar Reservoir, and the tranquil Quiet road of Piedra Caballera. The route also offers views of orange groves and the charming white houses of Restábal.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Lecrín Valley, where this route is located, enjoys an agreeable micro-climate, making it particularly pleasant for cycling in winter. Spring and autumn are also excellent, offering comfortable temperatures and lush landscapes.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Embalse de Béznar circular route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Restábal, a town of Arab origin. You should be able to find parking within the town, which serves as a convenient starting point for the circular trail.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route primarily follows roads and paved surfaces, it's generally not recommended for dogs due to the challenging nature for cyclists and potential traffic. If you do bring a dog, ensure it's well-behaved, on a leash, and capable of keeping up with the pace or being carried in a suitable bike trailer.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee to cycle this route or visit the Lecrín Valley?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route or to access the general area of the Lecrín Valley. It's open for public enjoyment.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other trails. You'll find it intersects with parts of the Ruta del Azahar, Andalucía - South Route (Provincia de Granada), Transandalus, and [CIMA GR13] Las Albuñuelas.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route starts and ends in Restábal, you'll find local cafes and restaurants in the town where you can refuel before or after your ride. The Lecrín Valley is known for its charming villages, many of which offer dining options.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating and length, we recommend bringing plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit for your bike, and appropriate cycling gear. Sunscreen and a hat are also advisable, especially during warmer months. Don't forget your camera for the stunning views!
